name: Talave Reservoir Loop from Hellín
name_original: Embalse del Talave — circular desde Hellín
description: Cycle a difficult 28.6-mile (46.0 km) loop from Hellín to the picturesque Talave Reservoir, gaining 1719 feet (524 metres).
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Hellín offer a diverse landscape for exploration, situated in the province of Albacete, Spain. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including river valleys, reservoirs, and agricultural areas. Cyclists can navigate paths alongside the Mundo and Segura rivers, explore areas near the Cancarix Volcano, and encounter features like the Los Almadenes Canyon. The landscape provides a mix of challenging ascents and more moderate, scenic routes. Best touring cycling routes around Hellín The most popular touring cycling route is Talave Reservoir Loop from Hellín, a 28.6 miles (46.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 56 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Talave Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hellín — circular route, a moderate 20.6 miles (33.2 km) path. This route provides a comprehensive tour of the Hellín area, suitable for a longer ride. Local touring cyclists also love the Embalse del Camarillas from Cancarix — circular gravel route through the Microrreserva Yesares de Hellín, a 33.0 miles (53.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Hellín is defined by river valleys, reservoirs, and the unique geological features of the Cancarix Volcano. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Hellín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 9 reviews. More than 290 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hellín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Hellín?
A: Hellín offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 options to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Hellín?
A: Yes, Hellín has several routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1065423962/tobarra-and-hellin-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobarra and Hellín Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 21.3 km path that offers scenic views and cultural stops, making it a good option for a family outing.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see on a touring bike ride in Hellín?
A: The region around Hellín is rich in diverse natural landscapes. You can cycle alongside the Mundo and Segura rivers, explore areas near the unique Cancarix Volcano, and encounter impressive features like the Los Almadenes Canyon. The routes also pass by reservoirs such as the Alfonso XIII Reservoir and picturesque 'bomba' rice fields.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Hellín?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Hellín are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1065439454/hellin-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hellín — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 33.2 km path that provides a comprehensive tour of the area.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Hellín offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1065407529/talave-reservoir-loop-from-hellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talave Reservoir Loop from Hellín&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 46.0 km trail with significant elevation gain, offering stunning views of the reservoir. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359317076/embalse-del-camarillas-from-hellin-round-trip-via-agramon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Camarillas from Hellín — round trip via Agramón&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 50 km.

Q: What cultural or historical sites can I visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: Hellín's routes can lead you to several cultural and historical sites. You might encounter the ancient Isso Roman Bridges, the beautiful Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope by the River Segura, or the significant El Tolmo de Minateda Regional Archaeological Park, known for its ancient settlement and cave paintings.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Hellín?
A: Hellín's climate generally allows for cycling throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any gravel touring routes in the Hellín area?
A: Yes, Hellín features gravel routes for those who prefer off-road touring. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068292986/embalse-del-camarillas-from-cancarix-circular-gravel-route-through-the-microrreserva-yesares-de-helin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Camarillas from Cancarix — circular gravel route through the Microrreserva Yesares de Hellín&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied terrain and unique geological features.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Hellín?
A: The touring cycling routes around Hellín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to reservoirs, and the unique geological features like the Cancarix Volcano, which make for a diverse and engaging riding experience.

Q: Are there any specific natural attractions or viewpoints to look out for?
A: Absolutely. Keep an eye out for the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Moratalla River and the Segura River&lt;/a&gt;, and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almacenes&lt;/a&gt;. These spots offer breathtaking views and unique natural beauty.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Hellín?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Hellín, as a city, offers various parking options. For routes starting directly from Hellín, you can typically find parking within the town or at designated spots near trailheads. For routes starting from smaller villages like Cancarix, local parking will usually be available.

Q: Can I reach touring cycling routes in Hellín using public transport?
A: Hellín is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to larger cities in the region. While public transport can get you to Hellín, accessing specific trailheads outside the city center might require further planning or a short ride to the starting point of your chosen route.
tips: A very scenic place, right next to the dam. The reservoir feeds the Mundo River.

It is a part of the Sendero del Río Mundo where it meets the road to the Talave reservoir. A very quiet path among the typical vegetation of the area, with views of the river and the surrounding mountains. The road in some parts is not in very good condition, but perfectly cyclable with a trekking, cross or gravel bike.

faqs.question: Where does the Embalse del Talave circular cycling route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular tour starting and ending in Hellín. You can typically find parking within Hellín itself, as it serves as the main access point for this tour.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain. You'll find a very quiet path alongside the river with typical local vegetation and mountain views. However, be aware that some sections of the road along this path are described as 'not in very good condition'. While some cycling in the broader region can involve sand and gravel, the primary access to the dam is via a service road.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Talave Reservoir, which is a very picturesque spot on the Mundo River. You'll also encounter sections of the Talave Road GR 67 Mundo Trail, offering natural appeal. Hellín itself, the starting point, has historical and cultural sites, including churches and sanctuaries.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the best conditions, spring and autumn are generally ideal for cycling in this region. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter, making for a more comfortable ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, it's primarily a touring bicycle route with varied road conditions. If you choose to bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved and kept on a leash, especially near roads or populated areas. Always carry water for your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Embalse del Talave circular route from Hellín. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially follows sections of the Sendero del Mundo and the GR-67 Sendero del Mundo, adding to its natural appeal and offering connections to other local trails.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The tour is approximately 46 kilometers long and is rated as 'difficult' for touring bicycles. Komoot estimates the duration to be around 2 hours and 57 minutes of active cycling time, but allow for extra time for breaks and enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What should I consider bringing for this touring bicycle trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, and appropriate clothing for the weather. Sun protection is also recommended, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route or in Hellín?
faqs.answer: While specific establishments directly on the route are not detailed, Hellín, as a town, offers various amenities including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's a good idea to stock up on supplies in Hellín before heading out.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'difficult' by Komoot, primarily due to its length (46 km) and some sections of road not being in very good condition. While manageable, beginners might find it challenging. It's best suited for cyclists with some experience and a bike capable of handling varied surfaces.
